title: Reconstruction Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For $n > 2$, two graphs $G$ and $H$ of order $n$ are isomorphic if and only if $\mathcal{D}(G) = \mathcal{D}(H)$.
context_text: Kelly and Ulam proposed this conjecture asking whether any graph on more than 2 vertices is uniquely determined (up to isomorphism) by its multiset of vertex-deleted subgraphs. The conjecture remains open, though it is known to hold for trees and for almost all graphs. The present paper studies a weaker reconstructibility question — recovering the number of edges from an incomplete deck — in this broader context.
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove new cases of the Reconstruction Conjecture, but establishes the related result that the number of edges of any graph on $n$ vertices is reconstructible from any deck missing at most $\frac{1}{20}\sqrt{n}$ cards (Theorem 1.2), contributing to the programme of partial-deck reconstruction.
notes: Section 3 of the paper is described as containing open problems ('some open problems are given in Section 3') but the full text of Section 3 is absent from the supplied PDF extraction; any conjectures or questions stated there could not be captured.
